# Fireball

- **Level**: 3
- **School**: Evocation
- **Casting Time**: 1 action
- **Range**: 150 feet
- **Components**: V, S, M (a tiny ball of bat guano and sulfur)
- **Duration**: Instantaneous
- **Classes**: Sorcerer, Wizard, Light Domain Cleric

## Description

A bright streak flashes from your pointing finger to a point you choose
within range, and then blossoms with a low roar into an explosion of
flame.  Each creature in a 20-foot-radius sphere centered on that
point must make a Dexterity saving throw.  A target takes 8d6 fire
damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

The fire spreads around corners.  It ignites flammable objects in the
area that are not being worn or carried.

## At Higher Levels

When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher,
the damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 3rd.

## Tactical Notes

- **AOE sizing**: A 20-foot radius covers a 41x41 foot square area on
  a battle grid.  Place the point of origin to maximize enemies caught
  while excluding allies.  Because the spread goes around corners, you
  can target a point just outside a doorway to hit creatures inside
  without exposing yourself to direct attack.
- **Material component trivia**: Bat guano and sulfur are cheap and
  non-consumed, so an arcane focus cannot substitute.  Always keep a
  pouch of the material on hand — losing it locks out the spell.
- **Damage type**: Fire is the most commonly resisted damage type in
  the game.  Against fiends and many undead, expect reduced effective
  output.  Sorcerers with Careful Spell metamagic can protect allies
  who would otherwise be caught in the blast.

## Cross-References

- See `fire-bolt.md` for the cantrip version of single-target fire
  damage.  Fireball trades single-target precision for AOE coverage.
- See `counterspell.md` — casting Fireball within 60 feet of an
  enemy caster exposes you to a reaction counterspell check.
